sourceMovie.title: The Driver
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Driver for the same reason it broke through in 1978: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Walter Hill keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Fast & Furious
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Driver clicked for you, Fast & Furious (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Driver?
faq.answer: Try Fast & Furious, Mesrine: Killer Instinct, Copshop—each shares DNA with The Driver while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Driver?
faq.answer: The Driver (1978) is best described as Crime, Thriller, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Driver worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
